title: Decisions on Syracuse budget cuts happened behind closed doors
summary: The Syracuse Common Council conducted a closed-door meeting on May 2nd with the Bonadio Group to review budget analysis recommending $26M in cuts, ultimately implementing $16M in reductions via 80 amendments. The non-public meeting, while technically legal as a caucus, has triggered transparency concerns from the mayor's administration and open government advocates who question whether budget decisions should occur outside public view.

entity_refs.name: New York Coalition for Open Government

political_signal.description: Council-executive branch tension over budget process legitimacy; open government advocates questioning procedural propriety of closed caucus meeting on budget matters; mayor's office formally objecting to exclusion from decision-making process.

processing_notes: Article date inconsistency: title says May 10, byline says May 9; using May 9 as publication date
No specific dollar amounts for cuts included in extracted text
Bonadio Group serves dual role as city auditor and budget analyst (potential conflict of interest not addressed in article)
Legal status of caucus meeting confirmed but advocacy position against its use for budget matters is clear
CIO should monitor for governance policy implications and potential IT/data governance intersections with audit process
